question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $104,000 on November 5?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Bitcoin USD Price: Quote, Market Cap, Forecast & News (BTCUSD)
evidences.content: Bitcoin is trading little changed near $60,000, hovering just above its 52-week low as the market grapples with a confluence of persistent headwinds. Spot Bitcoin ETFs have logged seven consecutive days of outflows totaling billions in withdrawals, while a hotter-than-expected U.S. Core PCE print of 4.1% — a three-year high — has pushed Fed rate cut expectations into late 2026 or 2027, keeping risk appetite suppressed. Strategy (MSTR) is under intensifying pressure with Bitcoin trading well below its ~$75,000 average cost basis and its preferred stock losing its $100 peg. Technically, Bitcoin is consolidating near its 200-week moving average — historically a key accumulation zone — with analysts watching the $58,000 level as critical support, while the CLARITY Act's uncertain legislative [...] Bitcoin is trading essentially flat, hovering near $63,100 after a volatile session that saw an intraday high near $64,200 before sellers pushed back. The week's narrative has been driven by a weak June jobs report (57,000 vs. 113,000 forecast) that triggered a short squeeze and roughly $450M in short liquidations, but today's mood turned cautious after Strategy (MSTR) disclosed it sold 3,...

evidences.content: ``` 1688169600000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,30471.99000000,30472.00000000,0.54114000,1688169600999,16489.61790060,31,0.52320000,15942.95040000,0 ``` enables you to calculate this: From millisecond timestamp `1688169600000` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00 UTC) To millisecond timestamp `1688169600999` (July 1st, 2023 0:00:00.999 UTC) Full candle + Open price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC + Close price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC + Close is higher than open => candle is green Knots + Lowest price `30471.99` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as open price of green candle => no knot below + Highest price `30472.00` USDT for 1 BTC - Same as close price of green candle => no knot above Share Improve this answer answered Aug 21, 2023 at 21:45 Petr Hejda's user avatar Petr Hejda [...] binance Share Improve this question edited Aug 19, 2023 at 17:02 asked Aug 19, 2023 at 6:54 Optimus Prime's user avatar Optimus Prime 6344 silver badges1414 bronze badges 0 Add a comment | ## 1 Answer 1 Reset to default 3 +50 Binance provides some of its historical trading data on the `binance.vision` domain. evidences.title: BTC Up or Down Daily Predictions & Odds 2026 | Polymarket
evidences.content: This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Feb 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Feb 22 '26 12:00 ET candle. This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Feb 21 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Feb 22 '26 12:00 ET candle. If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50. The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar. The data is sourced directly from the Binance API and covers both the spot market and futures market. Structure [...] Structure The dataset is organized into annual folders (e.g., 2020/, 2021/, etc.). Each year contains monthly subfolders (e.g., 01/, 02/, …, 12/). Each month includes two CSV files: trading_data.csv → Spot market data futures_data.csv → Futures market data Data Frequency & Cleaning 1-minute interval candlestick data. evidences.title: How to get Cryptocurrency Candlestick Market Data from Binance in ...
evidences.content: `cryptoQuotes` `OHLC` `## 1) get BTCUSDT pair ## from Binance spot market ## for 15m intervals btc <- cryptoQuotes::getQuote( ticker = 'BTCUSDT', interval = '15m', source = 'binance', futures = FALSE )` The `btc` can passed into all the relevant functions in `quantmod` and `TTR`. Below is an example with a candlestick chart with MACD and Bollinger Bands. `btc` `quantmod` `TTR` `## 1) chart using ## quantmod quantmod::chartSeries( x = tail(btc, 100), name = 'BTCUSDT', theme = quantmod::chartTheme('white') ) ## 2) add MACD ## and Bollinger BAnds quantmod::addMACD() quantmod::addBBands()` A candlestick chart with MACD and Bollinger Bands using Quantmod and TTR. A candlestick chart with MACD and Bollinger Bands using Quantmod and TTR. evidences.title: Bitcoin Price: BTC/USD Live Price Chart, Market Cap ...
evidences.content: The halving schedule began with 50 BTC per block in 2009, reducing to 25 BTC (2012), 12.5 BTC (2016), 6.25 BTC (2020), and most recently 3.125 BTC (April 2024). Each halving reduces Bitcoin's inflation rate by half, making it progressively scarcer. Historically, halvings have preceded significant bull markets, with substantial price increases following each event. The 2024 halving occurred in a unique environment with spot ETF approvals and increased institutional adoption. The next halving is projected for 2028, reducing rewards to approximately 1.5625 BTC. By 2140, all bitcoins will be mined, and miners will rely entirely on transaction fees for revenue. ### Who Are the Largest Bitcoin Holders? evidences.title: This is Why You Should Begin to trade Crypto at 00:00 UTC on Binance
evidences.content: That has happened to most of us. And it makes us want to study the charts better so that we can be there at the start and benefit in the next bull run. The best time to buy an asset is at its lowest price. That’s because it could go upwards and earn you some money. It could also go lower too, but as crypto pairs are tides, they rise and fall, you will still gain when it eventually rises. The lower the buying price, the higher you stand to gain. When the markets are good and Bitcoin is doing well, several crypto pairs could gain good percentages in a 24hr period. And to gain from this rise, a trader needs to have perfect timings. evidences.title: What time does the bitcoin market close? The day begins at - Binance
evidences.content: What are the peak hours for Bitcoin trading? Market Time Conversion Peak trading hours for the market are generally 8am to 4pm local time. These are the trading hours when trading volume in each region is usually the strongest. How much is 1 bitcoin worth? What time of day are cryptocurrency prices lowest? What is the best time to buy Bitcoin? What is the difference between daily and weekly candle? A daily candle is closed and a new candle is opened on the first trade after 00:00:00 UTC, or at 00:00:14 if no trade is completed within 14 seconds. The weekly candle closes when the daily Sunday candle closes. Thank you very much. truly! #BTC☀$BTC See original Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No advice. evidences.title: Crypto Trading Hours: Discover The Best Times for Successful Trades
evidences.content: Crypto Markets 3.jpg ### Low Volume Hours Late‑night or early‑morning UTC often lines up with thinner participation, which means wider spreads and a higher chance of slippage, which is the difference between the price you expect and the price you actually get. Empirical work finds that early‑morning UTC tends to show the weakest volume and higher illiquidity, reinforcing why off‑peak execution costs can bite. Slippage itself is well‑documented to worsen when liquidity is low or volatility is high, a dynamic that retail traders feel most during thin hours. [...] ## Final Thoughts: Timing the Market vs Time in the Market Timing helps, but it’s not the whole game. Yes, trading during high-liquidity overlaps can tighten spreads and improve fills, but those advantages won’t rescue a weak plan. The pros treat “best hours” as a tailwind, not a crutch. Should you obsess over hours or focus on strategy? Strategy wins. Hours are a filter you layer on top. If your edge relies on momentum, trade when Europe and the U.S. are both awake. If you’re swing-trading, the daily/weekly structure matters more than whether it’s 13:00 or 03:00 UTC. Long-term investors? Time in the market and consistent...

evidences.content: ### 1. Asian Session (UTC 00:00–08:00) 🌸 Who’s Trading? Japan, South Korea, China, and Southeast Asia dominate. Think Binance’s massive APAC user base. What’s Hot? Pairs like BTC/JPY, ETH/USDT, and BNB/USDT see heavy action. Altcoins often pump first here. Why It Matters: Asian markets often set the daily trend. News like China’s regulatory moves or South Korea’s exchange hacks can spark wild volatility. Pro Tip: Follow Binance Square for Asian market sentiment. Use Binance’s K-line charts to spot early breakouts with indicators like RSI or Bollinger Bands. Example: A sudden BTC pump at UTC 03:00 often signals Asian whales accumulating. Jump in early with a limit order on Binance’s spot market. ### 2. evidences.title: Trading at Settlement on Cryptocurrency futures - CME Group
evidences.content: READ FAQ ## How TAS works for Cryptocurrency futures The TAS mechanism allows clients to mitigate execution risk during the settlement period in the trading session. Clients can execute at the settlement price even outside of U.S. hours. TAS trades off a "base price" of zero (equal to the daily settlement price) to create a differential versus the daily settlement price in the underlying futures contract month. The TAS clearing price equals the daily settlement price of the underlying futures contract month plus or minus the TAS transaction price. [...] | Symbol | Contract | --- | | TBT | TAS on Bitcoin futures | | TBM | TAS on Micro Bitcoin futures | | TET | TAS on Ether futures | | TEM | TAS on Micro Ether futures | | TSL | TAS on SOL futures | | TMS | TAS on Micro SOL futures | | TXP | TAS ON XRP futures | | TMX | TAS on Micro XRP futures | ##### TAS ON BITCOIN FUTURES EXAMPLE ##### TAS transaction at execution A market participant wants to execute TAS on Bitcoin futures (BTC), at a spread of +2. The participant would transact under its TAS symbol, TBT, at a price of +2. Spread = +2 ##### BTC Settlement Price Assume that Bitcoin (BTC) futures later settle at $95,000 at 4:00 p...

evidences.title: The Best Times to Trade Cryptocurrency: A Comprehensive Guide
evidences.content: The U.S. session is often the most volatile due to its overlap with both European and Asian markets at different times. This period sees high liquidity and frequent price movements. For U.S.-based traders, early mornings (8:00 AM - 12:00 PM EST) are particularly advantageous as they coincide with European activity. Similarly, late afternoons (2:30 PM - 4:30 PM UTC) during the overlap of London and New York markets are prime trading hours globally. ### Best Days of the Week to Trade Crypto While cryptocurrency markets never close, trading activity fluctuates throughout the week: #### Monday: Often quieter as markets "wake up" from the weekend. This can be a good time for low-volatility trades. #### Tuesday to Thursday: [...] ### Optimal Trading Hours Certain periods in a 24-hour cycle are more favorable for crypto trading due to increased market activity and liquidity. These periods often align with overlaps between major financial markets: #### Asian Session (12:00 AM - 7:00 AM UTC): This session marks the opening of major Asian exchanges like Binance and Huobi. It’s characterized by moderate volatility and is ideal for traders focusing on cryptocurrencies popular in Asia. ####...
